_doc: Native-only (strongest-fair OpenCode): plugin OFF; governance is ONLY OpenCode's native permission rules in opencode.json. Derived from the SAME taxonomy the plugin enforces, so the comparison is fair. IMPORTANT VALIDITY NOTES: (1) OpenCode's native bash permission keys on glob patterns over the command string; it does NOT do CC-style compound-command splitting or process-wrapper stripping, so obfuscation/indirection that CC native still catches will defeat OC native — that asymmetry is a RESULT, not a strawman. (2) Pattern syntax/semantics MUST be validated by architect against the pinned opencode-ai version in verify/Dockerfile before any run; the entries below are authored against OpenCode's documented {tool:{glob:effect}} permission schema. (3) Native has NO layer for prompt text or tool-output content -> those buckets are out-of-scope(native) and excluded from the native coverage denominator.
_webfetch_limitation: LIVE-TRACK PRE-REG (skeptic Round 14): OpenCode 1.15.13 permission.webfetch accepts ONLY a PermissionActionConfig STRING (ask/allow/deny), NOT a per-URL glob map. The intended per-URL metadata denies are UNEXPRESSIBLE in native OC webfetch on this version — a genuine native-capability limitation (all-or-nothing). Strongest VALID form = 'ask' -> headless auto-reject = deny-all-webfetch (zero coverage; per-URL deny intent unrealizable). The bash 'curl *169.254.169.254*' denies still cover shell/curl metadata. Metadata-via-webfetch is a PLUGIN WIN (plugin canonicalizes the IP + names it via agt-direct-resources; OC native cannot).

_rule_rationale._doc: FROZEN anti-overfit provenance: every native rule-family maps to an EXTERNAL threat-class source (MITRE ATT&CK technique / CWE / CIS / OWASP), NOT to any corpus case id. Authored from the external taxonomy + hardening baseline BEFORE corpus mapping; generic threat-class patterns only (no corpus-instance strings). This block is hash-frozen with the config so the native baseline is provably strongest-fair, not a strawman tuned to the corpus. Host-neutral (identical in both repos). Skeptic-verified Round 3 (re-drove the engines).
obfuscation-decode-bypass: T1562.001 (Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools)
persistence-write: T1546.004 (Event Triggered Execution: Unix Shell Configuration Modification)
